methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Dusty Robotics, a Series B-stage robotics and manufacturing company, is evaluated against a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable in the robotics sector. The company has raised $70M+ to date. Valuation analysis employs two triangulation methods applicable to this stage and sector.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $18M total-raised proxy, implying a valuation of $34M. Peer-median analysis draws from six same-sector, same-stage comparables (1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, Fairmat, and one additional peer), yielding a median valuation of $1.1B with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology focus, market traction, and capital efficiency within frontier robotics.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $29M to $1.2B, with a median of $555M. The stage-progression floor reflects pure capital-deployment logic, while the peer median incorporates market pricing of comparable-stage robotics ventures. Key uncertainties include Dusty's revenue run rate and customer concentration relative to the peer set, neither disclosed in available data. The substantial valuation range underscores sensitivity to positioning within the robotics subsector—deployment and manipulation platforms command notably higher multiples than automation-focused peers. A credible point estimate requires clarity on revenue traction and competitive moat relative to the $1.6B–$10B tier of the peer set.
